The rendezvous server stores pagination cookies without bounds. An unauthenticated peer can repeatedly issue `DISCOVER` requests and force unbounded memory growth.

articles.summary:
### Summary

The`libp2p-rendezvous` server has no limit on how many namespaces a single peer can register. A malicious peer can repeatedly register unique namespaces in a loop, and the server accepts the requests, allocating memory for each registration without pushback. articles.summary:
Summary:
The file lightrag/api/config.py (line 397) uses a default JWT secret "lightrag-jwt-default-secret" when the TOKEN_SECRET environment variable is not set.
